Sender Name
James Beard Foundation
Sender Email
greens@jamesbeard.org
Campaign
Greens [CITY] — or — Greens x Young Patrons
Send 1 — Young Patrons (24-hr advance)
Audiences
  • Young Patrons Dynamic List
Send 2 — Greens Subscribers (24 hrs after YP)
Audiences

Send 3 — Final Reminder Evite
Audiences

West Coast (LA/SF/Seattle): We're still growing west coast Greens subscribers. For these cities, send to larger segmented lists PLUS "All Greens Subscribers." Suppress Young Patrons on the final reminder evite.

Email Campaigns
Always connect the correct campaign before scheduling. Find it in Basic Info → top right corner when editing the email in Pardot.
Campaign NameUsed For
Awards [year of cycle]Awards newsletter emails (open call, nominee announcements, etc.)
JBFA Evites and SurveysOne-off emails and invites to nominees, winners, and event attendees
Beard BitesAll Beard Bites newsletters
Industry SupportAll Industry Support newsletters
MarComms InitiativesBrand-focused marketing (e.g., new website announcement)
One-Off Programs EmailsApplication announcements, tickets to events (like FLW) going to Industry Support audience + other segments
Platform NewsletterAll weekly Platform newsletters
Platform Patron PreviewMonthly preview email exclusive to Patrons
Platform Special Events and PromotionsAdditional or special promotion of Platform events outside the newsletter
Patron NewsletterAll monthly Patron newsletters
Donor NewsletterAll monthly Donor newsletters
Development PromotionsOne-off emails promoting Patron membership offers or events
Taste AmericaLocal and national evites for each TA cycle
Greens [CITY]Evites to Greens subscribers (or full evite resends)
Greens x Young PatronsEvites to Young Patrons only
Community One-Off EmailsEvites or announcements for community programming
Legacy Network EmailsEvites or announcements for the Legacy Network
Active Drip Campaigns
Current automated welcome series in Pardot. These run automatically — do not manually add recipients to these without checking the triggers.
Beard Bites Welcome Series

Introduces new Beard Bites subscribers to JBF. Note: Likely to be renamed to a general welcome series as email strategy evolves.

All New Beard Bites Subscribers
  • 1. General welcome to JBF
  • 2. Introduction to Platform by JBF
  • 3. Promotion of JBF Patron program
Patron Program Welcome Series

Welcome series for all new JBF Patrons (recurring members do not receive this).

$500+ Level Patrons
  • 1. General thank you letter from Clare
  • 2. Details on Patron Program
  • 3. Intro to Platform + Patron-specific discount code
  • 4. General thank you / JBF overview
Young Patrons
  • 1. Details on Patron Program
  • 2. Intro to Platform + Patron-specific discount code
  • 3. General thank you / JBF overview
  • 4. Special welcome email from Aaron Hock
All Remaining Patrons
  • 1. Details on Patron Program
  • 2. Intro to Platform + Patron-specific discount code
  • 3. General thank you / JBF overview
Industry Welcome Series

Welcome series for new Industry Support subscribers.

All New Industry Support Subscribers
  • 1. General welcome to JBF (industry-facing version)
  • 2. Introduction to Platform by JBF
  • 3. Promotion of JBF Patron program
Schedule a Send
For emails already uploaded to Pardot drafts. Select the email type to pull in the correct sender info and audiences.
Quick Reference for Selected Email Type
Local Send Subject Lines
Fill in city, date, and days remaining — subject lines auto-populate.
Send 1
[CITY NAME]! Join Us for a Delicious Event on [DATE]
Send 2
Will You Join Us Next Month [Or in a Few Weeks] in [CITY NAME]?
Send 3
[XX] Days Left Until the Tastiest Night in [CITY NAME]!
Preview
View the menu and buy tickets now!
Steps
  1. 1
    Open the draft
    In Pardot, go to the Drafts page and click the email you want to send.
  2. 2
    Click "Edit draft"
    Click the Edit draft button at the top right of the screen.
  3. 3
    Update Basic Info → Campaign
    Click Basic Info (top right), update the campaign field to match the correct campaign, and hit Save.
  4. 4
    Go to the Sending tab
    Click the Sending tab in the middle of the page.
  5. 5
    Add audiences / lists
    Add the correct audience lists. Refer to the Quick Reference above for the right audiences for this email type.
  6. 6
    Update subject line
    Enter (or confirm) the subject line. For Taste America, use the subject line generator above.
  7. 7
    Confirm sender info
    Verify the Sender Name and Sender Email match what's in the Quick Reference above.
  8. 8
    Send a test (optional but recommended)
    Click the Testing tab and send a test version to yourself or relevant colleagues before scheduling.
  9. 9
    Schedule
    Back on the Sending tab, set your send date and time and schedule the email.
New Template Email
Creating a new email from a Pardot template (JBF letterhead or Beard Bites Platform banner).
Steps
  1. 1
    Start a new email
    On the Pardot Drafts page, click Send New List Email on the right side.
  2. 2
    Name it, set folder, assign campaign
    Enter a name for the email, select the correct folder (Scott usually creates these on the back end), and select the campaign.
  3. 3
    Select a template
    Choose the appropriate template: JBF letterhead template or Beard Bites Platform banner.
  4. 4
    Fill in text and images
    Edit text and images within the template. To upload an image: click into the block → click the photo icon → use the Upload tab or search in Image Info. Images should be sized 560 × 300px.
  5. 5
    Update the plain text version
    Use plain text to update the Text version of the email before sending.
  6. 6
    Add UTM links for all JBF website links
    For any link pointing to the JBF website, create a UTM link using the UTM Builder in the sidebar:
    • Source: email
    • Medium: newsletter name (e.g., beard-bites or industry-support)
    • Name: deployment date as YYYY-MO-DA (e.g., 2025-02-20)
    When inserting any link, set the target to "new window" in the Target tab.
  7. 7
    Add preview text
    Insert preview/preheader text. Reference: Pardot preview text guide.
  8. 8
    Insert subject line and sender info
    Enter the subject line and verify sender name + email match what's required for this email type.
  9. 9
    Select relevant audiences
    Add the correct lists. Reference the Recurring Newsletters or Other Email Types sections in the sidebar.
  10. 10
    Test the email
    Highly recommended: send a test version to yourself and any colleagues who need to review. Use the Testing tab.
  11. 11
    Schedule
    Once the test is approved, schedule accordingly on the Sending tab.
Upload Prospects & Create Lists
Importing contacts into Pardot and setting up static or dynamic segmented lists.
Upload Prospects from CSV
  1. 1
    Navigate to Import
    In the Pardot tab, hover over the toolbox icon or Admin at the bottom of the left menu → hover over Import → click Prospects.
  2. 2
    Select the upload method
    Click: "Create or update prospects using email address."
  3. 3
    Upload the CSV file
    Upload your CSV file, check the Compliance box, then click Next.
  4. 4
    Match fields
    Match the CSV columns to the correct Pardot fields, then click Next.
  5. 5
    Set campaign and tags (if applicable)
    If the list is related to a specific campaign, select it in the dropdown. Add any relevant tags. Click Next.
  6. 6
    Add contacts to a list
    Select: Import prospects and add to list(s) → select an existing list or create a new one. When creating a new list, do NOT check "Public List" — this would make contact info public.
  7. 7
    Confirm and save
    Confirm all information and click Save. Pardot will email you once all contacts have finished uploading.
Create a Dynamic Segmented List
  1. 1
    Navigate to Segmentation Lists
    In the Pardot tab, hover over the wand icon or Marketing at the top of the left menu → hover over Segmentation → click Lists.
  2. 2
    Click "Add List"
    Click the Add List button at the top right.
  3. 3
    Fill in name, folder, and check "Dynamic List"
    Enter a name and set the folder. Check the "Dynamic List" box to enable rule-based segmentation.
  4. 4
    Set dynamic list rules
    • Match all: every contact must satisfy all rules
    • Match any: contacts must satisfy at least one rule
    Click Add new rule: use Prospect default field for standard fields (city, zip, address, etc.). Click Add new rule group to create sub-groups with their own logic.
    Regional lists (by zip code): Use Prospect Default Field > Zip code > is > and enter all zip codes separated by semicolons (e.g., 11106;11107;11108…). Pro tip: Google a list of zip codes for the full county — that covers more area than the city alone.
  5. 5
    Click "Run Rules"
    Click Run Rules. Pardot will notify you by email once segmentation is complete.
Create a Salesforce Campaign
Creating a campaign record in Salesforce, which then becomes available to connect in Pardot.
Steps
  1. 1
    Open Salesforce Campaigns
    In Salesforce, select Campaigns from the top navigation bar.
  2. 2
    Click "New"
    Click the New button on the right side of the screen.
  3. 3
    Set record type to "Email Marketing"
    For email campaigns, the campaign record type must be Email Marketing. Click Next.
  4. 4
    Enter campaign name
    Type in the name. Keep it general — especially if it's part of a larger recurring campaign (e.g., "Beard Bites" rather than "Beard Bites March 2026").
  5. 5
    Set status
    Select the appropriate status: Planned, In Progress, Completed, or Aborted.
  6. 6
    Check "Active" and save
    Check the Active checkbox, then click Save. The campaign will take a few minutes to appear in Pardot.
